food_name: Bak Kut Teh — Singapore Teochew Style (1 claypot serving)

local_names.hindi: बाक कुट तेह (सूअर की पसली का सूप)

aliases: 肉骨茶 — 新加坡/潮州式 (Ròugǔchá — Xīnjiāpō/Cháozhōu shì)
बाक कुट तेह (सूअर की पसली का सूप)

chinese: 肉骨茶 — 新加坡/潮州式 (Ròugǔchá — Xīnjiāpō/Cháozhōu shì)
hindi: बाक कुट तेह (सूअर की पसली का सूप)

region_of_origin: Singapore (Teochew Chinese immigrant community, 19th century); also Johor, Malaysia
food_subgroup: Pork rib soup — clear peppery garlic broth with pork ribs, tofu puffs, optional fried dough stick

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Bak Kut Teh Singapore/Teochew style as a prepared hawker dish. Closest parent would be a pork rib soup composite dish; no exact FoodOn term available as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork rib soup, clear broth (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0JKG used as closest parent for clear pork rib soup. No exact FoodEx2 term for Singapore Teochew-style Bak Kut Teh.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this prepared hawker dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One claypot serving of approximately 600g comprising pork ribs (~200g with bone, ~120g meat), clear peppery garlic broth (~350ml), tofu puffs (~60g), and one optional fried dough stick (you tiao, ~30g), as served at Singapore hawker stalls such as Song Fa BKT and 965 BKT.

nutrient_source.title: Bak Kut Teh Teochew — HPB Singapore Foodo + Component Calculation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Teochew-style Bak Kut Teh, supplemented by component-level calculation for tofu puffs and fried dough stick. Per-100g values represent the composite dish (pork ribs, broth, tofu puffs, optional fried dough stick) weighted by portion mass. Trace minerals (copper, manganese) and vitamin K estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork rib and broth component data where HPB does not report these micronutrients.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Pork-Based Soup Dishes — Analogous Estimate Based on University of Sydney GI Database (pork-dominant, low-carbohydrate soup composite)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore Teochew Bak Kut Teh. GI estimated at ~55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous foods: the dish is dominated by pork protein and fat (GI-neutral), with the fried dough stick as the primary carbohydrate source (~14g per claypot). Fried dough (you tiao) has a GI of approximately 74–76 (University of Sydney); however, its contribution is diluted by the large protein-fat matrix of the dish, yielding a composite GI estimate of ~55. Without the fried dough stick, the effective GL drops to ~2. GL per 600g claypot = GI × available carbohydrate (g) / 100 = 55 × 15 / 100 = 8.25.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — approximately 700mg per claypot from broth and tofu puffs; dipping sauce adds a further 200–400mg
hypertension.guidance: One claypot of Teochew Bak Kut Teh contributes approximately 700mg sodium (35% of the WHO 2,000mg daily limit) before dipping sauce; patients with hypertension should avoid drinking the full broth and limit dark soy dipping sauce to reduce total sodium intake to below 1,000mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) and low glycemic load (~8 per claypot); carbohydrate content is minimal and dominated by the optional fried dough stick
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 55 and GL of approximately 8 per claypot, Teochew Bak Kut Teh is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes; omitting the fried dough stick reduces the GL to approximately 2, making it an excellent high-protein, low-glycaemic meal option.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork ribs (~12g per claypot) and moderate sodium; however, the dish is rich in protein and contains no trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume Teochew Bak Kut Teh in moderation due to approximately 12g saturated fat and 700mg sodium per claypot; choosing leaner rib cuts, avoiding the full broth, and omitting the fried dough stick will reduce saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No dong gui or anticoagulant herbs in Teochew style; pork rib collagen provides gelatin (hydroxyproline, glycine) but not at therapeutic joint-support doses
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Teochew Bak Kut Teh contains no dong gui (no anticoagulant herb interaction); white pepper (piperine) modestly enhances nutrient absorption, and the long-simmered pork rib collagen provides gelatin precursors, though not at clinically therapeutic doses for joint health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bak Kut Teh (肉骨茶, literally 'Meat Bone Tea') is an iconic Singapore hawker dish of Teochew/Hokkien Chinese heritage. The Singapore/Teochew version features a clear, intensely peppery and garlicky broth made by simmering meaty pork ribs for several hours with whole garlic cloves and a generous quantity of white peppercorns. This distinguishes it sharply from the Malaysian Klang/Hokkien style, which uses a dark, herb-intensive medicinal broth containing dong gui and other Chinese herbs. One claypot serving (~600g) comprises approximately 200g pork ribs with bone (~120g meat), 350ml clear broth, 60g tofu puffs, and one optional fried dough stick (you tiao, ~30g). Nutritional profile per claypot: approximately 510 kcal, 36g protein, 31g fat (12g saturated), 18g carbohydrate, and 700mg sodium (before dipping sauce). The glycemic index is approximately 55 (low) with a glycemic load of approximately 8 per claypot; omitting the fried dough stick reduces GL to approximately 2. The dish is dairy-free and high in protein. It is traditionally served with steamed white rice, dark soy sauce with sliced chillies for dipping the meat, and strong Oolong tea (the 'tea' in the name refers to the accompanying tea, not a soup ingredient). Clinically, the dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low GL; moderate sodium makes it a caution for hypertension patients who should avoid drinking the full broth; and moderate saturated fat warrants attention for cardiovascular disease patients. Key micronutrients include zinc (1.5mg/100g), vitamin B12 (0.4mcg/100g), niacin (2.0mg/100g), phosphorus (80mg/100g), and selenium (8mcg/100g). Source: HPB Singapore Foodo database. Iconic Singapore hawker stalls: Song Fa BKT (Clarke Quay), 965 BKT (Balestier), Ng Ah Sio BKT, Ya Hua BKT.
common_user_queries: teochew bak kut teh vs klang bak kut teh

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore dishes). Trace minerals (copper, manganese) and vitamin K estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork rib component data where HPB does not report these fields. GI estimated as analogous from University of Sydney GI Database. Overall confidence is high given the primary regional source match and well-documented dish composition.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite Singapore hawker dish not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central composite entry as of 2026-04-14. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite claypot dish (broth + solids) not separately reported in HPB Foodo as of 2026-04-14. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Singapore Foodo data for this dish. 4. Copper (0.08mg/100g), manganese (0.05mg/100g), and vitamin K (0.1mcg/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork rib and broth component data (confidence 0.9) as HPB Foodo does not report these micronutrients for this dish. 5. GI of ~55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Singapore Teochew Bak Kut Teh exists in the published literature as of 2026. 6. Sodium values reflect the composite dish per 100g; total claypot sodium is approximately 700mg before dipping sauce. Dipping sauce (dark soy + chilli) adds approximately 200–400mg sodium per serving. 7. The fried dough stick (you tiao) is optional; without it, carbohydrate per claypot drops from ~18g to ~4g and GL drops from ~8 to ~2. 8. FoodEx2 code A0JKG used as closest parent (pork rib soup, clear broth); no exact FoodEx2 term for Singapore Teochew Bak Kut Teh exists.
